---
title: "Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation"
description: "Study note on Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation in Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "handbook-practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "150-151"
citation_key: "newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch05-chapter-5-performance-measurement:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 150-151</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation" from Kathryn E. Newcomer, Harry P. Hatry, and Joseph S. Wholey's *Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation* [@newcomer_hatry_wholey_2015_handbook_practical_program_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The chapter treats performance measurement as both an evaluation tool and a management system.

## Study Summary

The chapter treats performance measurement as both an evaluation tool and a management system.

Ongoing measures can strengthen decision making, but they need evaluation thinking to avoid becoming mechanical reporting.

The main issue is how to design and implement systems that focus on results and provide useful feedback.

## Key Concepts

- Performance measurement
- Monitoring system
- Outcome focus
- Feedback

## Source Location

- Section path: Part 1: Evaluation Planning and Design > Chapter 5: Performance Measurement
- Evidence records: `chunk:handbook-practical-program-evaluation:ch05-chapter-5-performance-measurement:c0001`

## Practical Implications

- Design performance measures around decisions and learning needs.
- Use evaluation methods to improve measure validity and interpretation.
- Connect monitoring trends to deeper evaluative questions when needed.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
